mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets price slower policy normalization and easier real rates; equities re-rate upward over the next month, implying a positive 20-day S&P 500 return.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with flat-to-falling FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL produce negative 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the sign flips after accounting for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market cooling and weaker wage pressure; because the market anticipates easier future policy, SPX tends to rise over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp versus the prior 2-month average
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE trigger events are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 5-day return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger RSAFS indicates resilient nominal consumer demand, which supports earnings expectations and keeps recession fears low; this raises SPX over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained gains in RSAFS are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upside move in INDPRO alongside stable PAYEMS indicates better productivity and supply-side growth rather than overheating demand; that mix supports equity margins and produces positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while PAYEMS growth stays nonnegative
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO/PAYEMS trigger does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered episodes yield negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent and CPIAUCSL is still rising, discount rates stay restrictive while inflation remains sticky, causing equities to de-rate and fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate high-inflation trigger episodes do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is above threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month rise in PAYEMS with UNRATE unchanged or lower signals broadening job creation without rising slack, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues; SPX should rise over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if labor gains with stable-to-lower unemployment fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses during soft-landing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend reflects weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical activity; this depresses equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing contraction triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the average 60-day return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive in the latest GDPC1 release and retail sales RSAFS are also rising, aggregate demand momentum is firm enough to lift earnings expectations, generating positive SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDP and retail-strength episodes do not outperform either proxy alone, or if the combined signal fails to deliver positive 20-day SPX returns.
